commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 22. "Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me" (ver. 13). As if he were saying, "`If Thou wilt, Thou canst make me clean.' Be pleased to deliver me. O Lord, look upon me to help me." Look, that is, on the penitent members, members that lie in pain, members that are writhing under the instruments of the surgeon; but still in hope.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Be pleased. The rest is nearly transcribed, Psalm lxix. (Calmet)
The Church prays for her weak members. (Worthington)
